name: boondocking near here
description: "You can bookdock right outside yosemite, ca close to the west entrance in the national forest free for up to 14 days. We'll it's that way for most NF and BLM that you can stay free up to 14 days in one area. You go about 1/4 mile from the entrance, then turn to the right where they have snow plow equipment. This area is popular. Yosemite is amazing though just stunning. CA gas is expensive so gas up in whatever state you are in before CA."

name: Mammoth Lakes
description:
This might be might favorite mountain area I've been to yet, out of 40 something states. California nature continues to amaze me.
Gorgeous area in the Sierra Nevada mountains, shaded and much cooler weather than the 100+ degree weather elsewhere. I found multiple hot springs you could sit in like a hot tub, trails absolutely everywhere, boondocking everywhere

Badger Flats Dispersed
Pike National Forest
Colorado / Lake George
Best of the Area on CR-31 East of Wilkerson Pass
Lake George, Colorado
GPS: 39.05392, -105.46626
Elevation: 8727'
Public - Forest Service (Official)
The road in is Gravel and 1 mile miles from a paved road. There are 30 or more campsites at this location and the maximum RV length is unlimited. You may stay 14 days at Best of the Area on CR-31 East of Wilkerson Pass.
Activities:
RV Parking
Tent Camping
OHV
Biking
Hiking
Horse Trails
Wildlife Viewing
Submitted by Anonymous. Last update: May 8, 2017
Dozens and Dozens of possible campsites once you enter NFS land at 39.04303 -105.45502 on CR-31 between Lake George and Wilkerson Pass. I found an 'easy' site for RV at 39.05128 -105.45956 but if you return time after time you can 'discover' Many sites down many little dirt roads off CR-31 that are good for RV, some of which you will need to be 'brave' to get to but work Great once there. CR-31 is an Excellent maintained gravel road.
Been here? Rate it!
Because of its relatively easy access and numerous campsites, we chose Badger Flats as the location for our first boondocking experience. There are three excellent fisheries close by all of which are fed by the South Platte River. A short 20-minute drive westward you’ll find Spinney Reservoir, The Dream Stream and Eleven Mile Reservoir. These fisheries provide access to cutthroat, rainbow and brown trout as well as northern pike and even Kokanee salmon.
As we turned off highway 24, we immediately saw No Camping signs but soon realized this applies only to the state-owned land which borders the highway. One mile further up CR 31, we reached the federal land where camping is allowed. The side roads are flat and in reasonable shape which allowed us to tow our 22-foot trailer without concern. There are likely more than a hundred campsites scattered throughout the area some in the trees and some more exposed. Riders of off-road vehicles are aware of Badger Flats. Everyone was considerate always slowing down when approaching a campsite. Target shooting occurred throughout the day which might be a consideration for some.
Our cell phones had a difficult time connecting to a Verizon signal despite the close proximity of a cell phone tower on top of Badger Mountain. Signal strength was 1-2 bars at best. We rarely received a 3G carrier.
Being near the top of Wilkerson Pass, the area can become rather windy at times. We took advantage of some calmer weather on day two to take a short hike during which we spotted a badger crossing the OHV trail. We kept our distance as he intently watched us from his home which was less than 10 feet from the trail. Not sure who named Badger Flats or when, but it was fun to see they are still doing well there today.

name: Cave-In-Rock State Park
description: there are camping accommodations on the scenic north side of the park. There are 34 Class A sites that rent for $20 a night, and $30 a night on holiday weekends/weekdays (Memorial Day, July 4th and Labor Day). Each is equipped with electricity and can accommodate units up to 60-feet long. Twenty-five Class B/S tent sites also are available for $10 a night, and showers, restrooms and dumping stations are present in both camping areas. Contact the site superintendent or campground host for camping permits and information. Accessible sites also are available. Grills are furnished at each campsite.

name: Cedar Lake
description:
Directions:
From Murphysboro: Take Highway 127 south 10 miles to Boat Dock Road. Turn east (left) onto Boat Dock Rd., continue 0.8 mile to the trailhead.
From Jonesboro: Take Highway 146 west 2.5 miles, to Hwy 127, then north 12 miles on Hwy 127 to Boat Dock Road. Turn east (right) onto Boat Dock Rd., continue 0.8 mile to the trailhead.
General Notes:
Primitive camping is allowed up to 14-days continuous stay. Set up campsite at least 150 feet from trails, trailheads and lakes or streams. Drown campfires completely and naturalize your campsite by practicing Leave No Trace Outdoor Ethics.

name: Chain O'Lakes State Park
description: Chain O’ Lakes has Class A-Premium areas (Honey Suckle Hollow and Fox Den) with a total of 151 sites. Sites are for $25 per night, except on Memorial Day, July 4th and Labor Day weekends when the fee is $35 per night. Three Class B-Premium camp areas (Turner Lake South, Prairie View and Mud Lake East) have a total of 87 sites for $12 each per night. One youth group camp area (Mud Lake West) will accommodate 150 youths with a fee of $2 per person (minimum $20 per night). Maximum stay at campsites is 14 nights in a 30 day period. A responsible adult (18 years of age or older) acquiring a permit must have a camp shelter and set up at the time of registration. All campers must have a permit (receipt for payment). Gathering of firewood from the park is strictly prohibited. Alcohol is prohibited in the campgrounds.

name: Fort Kaskaskia Campground
description: There is Camping at Fort KasKaskia. Please contact the Site at 618/859-3741. For other information about Fort Kaskaskia visit our website.

name: Fort Massac State Park
description: Campers enjoy Fort Massac's 50 Class A vehicular campsites, complete with electricity, a disposal station and a shower building. Tent camping and a separate group campground also are available. Camping reservations can be made through ExploreMoreIL™.

name: Fox Ridge State Park
description: Camping at Fox Ridge offers plenty of amenities. Picnic tables, cooking grills, drinking water, toilet facilities and a sanitary dumping station are available to campers. The shower building is closed by November 1 (may be earlier if bad weather) and reopen April 15 (may be earlier - weather depending). Reservations are available at ExploreMoreIL™.

name: Garden of the Gods Recreational Area
Giant City State Park
description: Giant City is a popular weekend retreat because of its varied and well-kept campground facilities. There are 85 campsites in the Class A Campground which provides water, electricity, showers and sanitary facilities for tent and trailer camping. The shower building in the Class A site closes the day after the first shotgun deer season (weather depending) and re-opens April 1st.

name: Green River State Wildlife Management Area
description: Camping spaces with vehicular access are provided. A sanitary dump station also is available.

name: Hamilton County - ?? not sure where in County
description: Tent and trailer sites are available with electricity, sanitary dump stations and a shower house. Campers without reservations need to obtain a permit from the site personnel when they arrive at the area. Five cabins are located on scenic Dolan Lake across from a modern shower building. No cooking, pets or smoking allowed in the cabins. Fire grills and tables are provided out side each cabin. Full-size bed, two sets of bunk beds, and a table and chairs are provided inside. Cabins have heat and air conditioning. Bring your own bedding and mattress. There must be an adult (18 or older) to stay in the cabin or camping areas. For reservations of the campsites or cabins, visit ExploreMoreIL™.

name: Lake of Egypt
description:
Directions:
From Creal Springs, IL take Creal Springs Road south 3 miles to Recreation Road. Turn right (west) onto Recreation Road and follow the signs 3 miles to the boat launch.General Notes:
Overnight parking is allowed along the side of Forest Service Roads, however do not block road or gates or cause resource damage.
Camping Regulations in General Forest Areas: 
Primitive camping is allowed ¼ mile outside developed areas within Shawnee National Forest. There is no fee for primitive camping and it’s allowed any time of year. Don’t trespass on private land, and do not cut down live trees, shrubs or other vegetation. Camp and wash at least 150 feet from any water source. Pack out your garbage and make sure campfires are completely out.
Campers may camp up to 14-days in one location. Camping is not allowed in the 80 designated natural areas or within 150 feet of lakes and streams or near trails.

name: Pyramid State Recreation Area

There are three Class C camping areas, Class D hike-in campsites, a small equestrian camp and a youth group camping area. Water and a trailer dump station are available near the site office. There are no electrical hookups in the camping areas.

description: Nestled in Winnebago County, Sugar River Forest Preserve is a 500+ acre county forest with traditional campgrounds and a primitive campground right along the namesake river. Fall asleep to the sound of rushing waters after hauling your gear a short distance from the parking lot. These primitive campgrounds still come with picnic tables and a fire pit but you’ll have to drive back to the main campgrounds if you want to access the showers!

name: Trail of Tears State Forest
description: Both Class C (tent camping with vehicle access) and Class D (backpack) camping sites are available at Trail of Tears State Forest. Some locations have log shelters with adjacent privies. Group camping is available at a few sites. The forest's gravel roads are closed to vehicles from December 24 through the end of the spring wild turkey hunting season (

name: Weinberg-King State Fish and Wildlife Area
Weldon Springs State Park
description:
Weldon Springs’ campground is open year-round; the shower building closes in the fall and reopens in the spring. It offers 75 Class A sites with electricity, vehicular access, sanitary dumping station, shower building, cooking grills, picnic tables, pit toilets. Playground equipment and water hydrants are available in the campground. Campers with disabilities have access to three campsites designed for special needs offer a large hard-surfaced pad. Privies and water are easily accessible. Areas for tent, backpack, large group and youth camping are also available. Primitive backpack campsites are located along Salt Creek. Please call ahead for conditions, as these backpack sites could be flooded in spring. The shower buildings are closed by November 1 (maybe earlier if bad weather persists) and reopen May 1 (maybe earlier if weather allows). Camping and Group Camp Reservations are accepted online at
ExploreMoreIL™.
